Component.Name: MissionControlStateMachine
Component.Purpose: Top-level mission control component coordinating initialization, exploration, return-to-start, and second run. Owns the Exploration State Machine and drives other modules (frontier generation, path planner, motion control, return-to-start, second-run planner) in the periodic control loop. Ensures deterministic transitions among mission phases and enforces exploration stop conditions (map complete or time limit).

Signal.Name: INIT_COMPLETED
Signal.Description: Boolean flag or event published once initialization of hardware and software is complete. Accessible as a latched boolean in the control-loop context. MissionControlStateMachine reads this flag; it is true for all subsequent cycles after initialization. No further payload.

Signal.Name: SensorFrame
MapCompletenessFlag
Signal.Description: Boolean value map_complete provided each control loop cycle. True iff no cell or inter-cell wall segment is UNKNOWN. Used by MissionControlStateMachine to trigger transition from EXPLORE to RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: MapQueryRequest
Signal.Description: Synchronous function-style calls from MissionControlStateMachine to query: - start_cell coordinate - goal_region cells Primarily used for ReturnToStart and SecondRun state actions. Implemented as direct function calls to GridMapModule APIs.

Signal.Name: FrontierSet
Signal.Description: On request, returns a list/vector of grid coordinates (row, col) representing frontier cells: UNKNOWN cells with at least one FREE 4-neighbor. MissionControlStateMachine uses this set to select next exploration targets via its heuristic.

Signal.Name: FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Function call invoked by MissionControlStateMachine while in EXPLORE. Parameters: none (uses current map). Returns FrontierSet synchronously.

Signal.Name: PlannedPath
Signal.Description: Sequence of cell coordinates representing a path: array/list of (row, col) from given start to target/goal. Also includes total_traversal_cost (float) and validity flag. Used in EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN.

Signal.Name: PlanningFailure
Signal.Description: Boolean or enumerated status returned or raised by planner when no feasible path exists between current cell and requested target/goal region. MissionControlStateMachine uses this to handle RETURN_TO_START and SECOND_RUN failure statuses.

Signal.Name: PlanRequest
Signal.Description: Function-style calls: - plan_to_frontier(current_cell, frontier_cell, heuristic_mode=EXPLORATION) - plan_to_start(current_cell, start_cell) - plan_to_goal_region(start_cell, goal_region, traversal_cost_profile) Called by MissionControlStateMachine depending on state.

Signal.Name: MotionCommand
Signal.Description: High-level execution command describing: - Path: ordered list of cell centers to traverse - Mode: EXPLORATION or SECOND_RUN or RETURN - Start index along current path MotionControlModule converts to primitive motor_set sequences respecting rate limits and safety. MissionControlStateMachine triggers when a new path is selected or when replanning happened.

Signal.Name: StopCommand
Signal.Description: Command instructing MotionControlModule to stop issuing further movement primitives and to bring robot to rest (motor_set(0,0) while respecting rate limiting unless emergency). Used in COMPLETED or when entering safe states.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Information on current path execution: - current_cell (row, col) - segment_index - reached_target (bool) - aborted (bool) due to collision threat or emergency MissionControlStateMachine uses this to know when exploration target reached, when start cell reached, and when second run result is final.

Signal.Name: ReturnPath
Signal.Description: Similar to PlannedPath but specifically from current cell to start cell. If no path, indicates planning failure.

Signal.Name: ReturnToStartRequest
Signal.Description: Invoked from MissionControlStateMachine on entering RETURN_TO_START after EXPLORATION_COMPLETED or EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT. Includes current_cell as parameter.

Signal.Name: SecondRunPath
Signal.Description: Fastest path from start cell to selected goal region. Includes traversal cost and validity. Built with traversal cost model tuned for minimum time.

Signal.Name: SecondRunStatus
Signal.Description: Enumeration {SUCCESS, FAILURE, ABORTED_INCOMPLETE}. Provided upon completion of second-run motion execution; latched by MissionControlStateMachine in COMPLETED state.

Signal.Name: SecondRunPlanRequest
Signal.Description: Invoked by MissionControlStateMachine upon entering SECOND_RUN. Includes start_cell and goal_region definition. Returns SecondRunPath synchronously or via callback before execution begins.

Signal.Name: EXPLORATION_COMPLETED
Signal.Description: Event enqueued when grid map completeness criterion is first met. Contains timestamp_ms and final map completeness flag. Consumed by MissionControlStateMachine to transition from EXPLORE to RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT
Signal.Description: Event enqueued when 5-minute global exploration limit reached while EXPLORE is active. Contains timestamp_ms. Causes MissionControlStateMachine to terminate EXPLORE and initiate RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Implicit periodic invocation of MissionControlStateMachine::update() at configured control loop frequency, synchronized with sensor sampling. Provides current time (ms since mission start) and cycle index. MissionControlStateMachine uses this for time-aware planning and exploration timeout evaluation.

Component.Execution: Executed once per iteration of the periodic control loop (Scheduler). Each update() call: 1. Reads INIT_COMPLETED flag. 2. Reads SensorFrame validity, exploration status events, map completeness flag, and elapsed time since exploration start. 3. Advances internal mission state machine deterministically based on current state and inputs. 4. When required by state, issues requests to FrontierGeneration, PathPlanner, ReturnToStartService, SecondRunPlanningService. 5. Issues MotionCommand/StopCommand to MotionControlModule corresponding to active path. Preconditions: - Initialization module has set up hardware and initial software structures. - GridMapModule and PoseEstimation are functional.
Component.Timing: - Executed at control loop frequency f in [10, 50] Hz, matching sensor sampling (requirement LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 and LL--SCHED--1.1.1). - State transitions that depend on events (INIT_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT, path completion) are evaluated within a single control-loop update; worst-case latency is one period T. - Frontier requests and path planning may be performed over multiple cycles if required for performance; however, decisions remain deterministic: same inputs sequence yields identical state trajectory. - Exploration timeout: global limit of 5 minutes (300000 ms) from exploration_start_time; MissionControlStateMachine records exploration_start_time at transition IDLE->EXPLORE.
Component.Invariants: - Mission state is always one of {IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, COMPLETED}. - IDLE may only transition to EXPLORE once per mission. - Once COMPLETED is entered, no further transitions occur; no new MotionCommand is issued, only StopCommand if needed. - At most one active path is tracked at a time; when a new path is accepted, previous path is abandoned. - During RETURN_TO_START and SECOND_RUN, commanded targets never correspond to cells classified as BLOCKED.
Component.Failures: - Planning failure in RETURN_TO_START: Trigger: ReturnToStartService signals planning failure (no path to start). Handling: MissionControlStateMachine transitions directly to COMPLETED and records mission result as "return_to_start_failed". Issues StopCommand to MotionControlModule. Second run is not initiated. - Planning failure in SECOND_RUN: Trigger: SecondRunPlanningService cannot compute a path to the goal region. Handling: MissionControlStateMachine transitions to COMPLETED with second_run_status=FAILURE and issues StopCommand. - Exploration unable to find new frontier: Trigger: FrontierGeneration returns empty FrontierSet while map is not yet complete and before timeout. Handling: MissionControlStateMachine triggers EXPLORATION_COMPLETED via ExplorationStatusModule (or treats as map logically complete), then proceeds to RETURN_TO_START. - Any emergency stop during RETURN_TO_START or SECOND_RUN causing path execution abort: Trigger: MotionControlModule reports aborted==true due to emergency stop. Handling: MissionControlStateMachine requests replanning from current_cell. If repeated replanning fails, mission terminates in COMPLETED with second_run_status=FAILURE or return_to_start_failed accordingly.

Component.Name: GridMapModule
Component.Purpose: Maintains the in-memory 4x8 grid map representation of the maze, including cell and inter-cell wall classifications. Provides single-threaded query/update API and change-notification events to other modules. Implements initialization, start cell setup, static map immutability, map completeness criterion, and grid coordinate system.

Signal.Name: CurrentCellAndHeading
Signal.Description: Estimated discrete pose: { uint8 row; uint8 col; enum Heading {N,E,S,W} heading; bool at_cell_center; }. Provided each control loop iteration. Used to index map cells and walls during updates.

Signal.Name: CellCenterTransitionEvent
Signal.Description: Event emitted when pose crosses from one cell center to adjacent cell center. Contains old_cell, new_cell, and direction. GridMapModule uses this to mark traversed cells as FREE and update walls on the entry face.

Signal.Name: WallObservation
MapCompleteFlag
Signal.Description: Boolean indicating whether all cells and inter-cell walls have non-UNKNOWN states. Computed on each update or incrementally maintained. Exposed via getter or event to MissionControlStateMachine and ExplorationStatusModule.

Signal.Name: StartCellCoordinate
Signal.Description: Fixed (row, col) designating start cell. Default (0,0) as per grid coordinate system. Used by ReturnToStartService and SecondRunPlanningService.

Signal.Name: MapQueryInterfaceForFrontiers
Signal.Description: Provides bulk or per-cell access to cell classifications and 4-neighborhood relationships, enabling efficient recomputation of frontier sets. FrontierGenerationModule registers for map-change notifications.

Signal.Name: FrontierSubscription
Signal.Description: Subscription request to be notified when any UNKNOWN cell or wall becomes FREE/BLOCKED/PRESENT/ABSENT. Notifications carry affected cell/wall indices.

Signal.Name: MapQueryInterfaceForPlanner
Signal.Description: APIs: - get_cell_state(row,col): returns enum CellState - get_wall_state_between(cell_a, cell_b): returns enum WallState - iterate_free_cells() These functions are read-only and executed in control-loop context.

Signal.Name: CellStateSnapshot
Signal.Description: Provides snapshot of FREE/BLOCKED classifications to search for reachable 2x2 FREE regions from the start cell. GoalRegionSelector uses this in conjunction with PathPlanner.

Component.Execution: - Initialized during system startup: 1. Define grid size (4 rows x 8 columns). 2. Initialize all cells and inter-cell walls to UNKNOWN. 3. Mark start cell (default (0,0) but configurable) as FREE. - At each control loop: 1. Consume CellCenterTransitionEvent(s) from PoseEstimationModule and mark traversed cells as FREE if movement completed without collision. 2. Consume WallObservation(s) from WallDetectionModule, apply consistency checks (delegated to Reliability/SensorConsistency), and update wall segment states (UNKNOWN -> PRESENT/ABSENT). 3. Compute or update map completeness state: - Track count of UNKNOWN cells and UNKNOWN walls; mark map_complete when both counts reach 0. 4. Dispatch classification-change notifications to registered subscribers (frontier generation, path planner if needed).
Component.Timing: - All read/write operations executed within main control-loop thread (LL--MAP--1.3.7), guaranteeing no additional synchronization needed. - Map completeness evaluation runs each cycle or incrementally on each update; with small 4x8 grid, a full scan (cells+walls) within each control loop is acceptable.
Component.Invariants: - Every cell has exactly one classification in {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}. - Every wall segment between valid adjacent cells has exactly one classification in {UNKNOWN, PRESENT, ABSENT}. - Transitions: * Cell: UNKNOWN -> FREE or UNKNOWN -> BLOCKED only, never back to UNKNOWN and never FREE<->BLOCKED. * Wall: UNKNOWN -> PRESENT or UNKNOWN -> ABSENT only, never back to UNKNOWN and never PRESENT<->ABSENT. - Grid dimensions remain fixed at 4x8 for entire mission.
Component.Failures: - Inconsistent update attempt: * Trigger: external module attempts to set a cell or wall to a value violating allowed transitions (e.g., FREE->BLOCKED). * Handling: GridMapModule rejects the update and logs an internal error; classification remains unchanged. - Out-of-bounds access: * Trigger: query or update with indices outside [0..3]x[0..7]. * Handling: operation returns error code or safe default (UNKNOWN) and no internal state is modified.

Component.Name: FrontierGenerationModule
Component.Purpose: Maintains and provides the set of frontier cells used for frontier-based exploration. A frontier cell is currently UNKNOWN and is a 4-neighbor of at least one FREE cell. Recomputes or incrementally updates frontier set whenever relevant cell classifications change. Serves frontier requests from MissionControlStateMachine and planner.

Signal.Name: FrontierSet
Signal.Description: Set (list) of coordinates (row, col) where each cell is UNKNOWN and has at least one FREE cell in its 4-neighborhood. Precomputed and cached; recomputed on demand or maintained incrementally.

Signal.Name: MapChangeNotification
Signal.Description: Event emitted when a cell or wall classification changes. Contains: - type: CELL or WALL - index: row, col, and/or adjacent cell pair - old_state, new_state. FrontierGenerationModule uses cell classification changes relevant to UNKNOWN->FREE/BLOCKED to update frontier set.

Signal.Name: MapQueryAccess
Signal.Description: Access to cell classifications via GridMapModule API: get_cell_state(row,col), neighbor traversal. Used to evaluate frontier definition.

Signal.Name: FrontierSetResponse
Signal.Description: Response to FrontierRequest. Contains current frontier set and possibly precomputed heuristic attributes (e.g., estimated number of new cells observable per frontier, Manhattan distance from current cell).

Signal.Name: FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Request from mission control or planner asking for up-to-date frontier set, optionally with: - current_cell coordinate - remaining_time_ms FrontierGenerationModule may attach simple precomputed features used by the selection heuristic.

Component.Execution: - On initialization: compute initial frontier set over entire 4x8 grid: for each cell UNKNOWN: if any 4-neighbor is FREE: add to frontier set. - On MapChangeNotification where a cell state changes UNKNOWN->FREE or UNKNOWN->BLOCKED: - For the updated cell: * If it was a frontier, remove from frontier set. - For its 4-neighbors: * If neighbor is UNKNOWN: - If new FREE neighbor: neighbor may become a frontier cell; add if not present. - If neighbor lost last FREE neighbor due to re-classification (not allowed by immutability) this cannot occur; static assumption holds. - On FrontierRequest: - Return current cached frontier set. - Optionally compute for each frontier: * Manhattan distance from current robot cell. * Estimate of newly observable UNKNOWN cells based on local UNKNOWN cluster around frontier.
Component.Timing: - All updates and queries are performed synchronously inside the control-loop thread. - Frontier set maintenance complexity is O(1) per map update for neighbors plus O(F) per full recomputation, where F is number of cells. With fixed 32 cells, unacceptable overhead is unlikely.
Component.Invariants: - Every cell in the frontier set: * is UNKNOWN; * has at least one 4-neighbor cell classified FREE. - No cell classified FREE or BLOCKED can be part of frontier set.
Component.Failures: - FrontierSet empty while map is not complete: * Trigger: frontier set is empty and GridMapModule reports map_complete==false. * Handling: module returns empty set; MissionControlStateMachine or ExplorationStatusModule may interpret this as completion or unreachable areas depending on policy.

Component.Name: ExplorationTargetSelector
Component.Purpose: Implements the heuristic for prioritizing frontier targets. Uses planner's traversal cost model to estimate travel time and a visibility heuristic to favor targets expected to reveal many new cells, while ensuring target exploration fits within remaining exploration time budget. Returns a single selected frontier cell or indicates that no acceptable target exists.

Signal.Name: FrontierHeuristicInputs
Signal.Description: Receives the current list of frontier cells plus optional precomputed local-unknown-counts or other features from FrontierGenerationModule to reduce recomputation.

Signal.Name: FrontierSet
Signal.Description: Set of frontier cells to be evaluated by heuristic. Also contains per-cell adjacency info required to compute neighborhood UNKNOWN counts if not provided precomputed.

Signal.Name: TravelCostEstimates
Signal.Description: For each candidate frontier cell, ExplorationTargetSelector queries the planner's cost model to evaluate estimated travel-time component: - cost(current_cell, frontier) using same edge cost parameters as used in actual path planning (straight and turn penalties).

Signal.Name: HeuristicCostQuery
Signal.Description: API to compute approximate cost metrics (e.g., potential path length or Dijkstra precomputation) without computing actual full path. Alternatively, selector can request full path costs but may limit calls for performance.

Signal.Name: SelectedTarget
Signal.Description: Chosen frontier cell (row, col) and associated estimated time_to_reach_ms and expected_newly_observable_cell_count. If no frontier satisfies remaining time constraint, SelectedTarget contains invalid flag.

Signal.Name: TargetSelectionRequest
Signal.Description: Contains: - current_cell - remaining_exploration_time_ms - time_per_cell_ms (from Nominal Cell Traversal Duration Parameter) MissionControlStateMachine uses SelectedTarget to then request path planning and motion execution.

Component.Execution: - For each TargetSelectionRequest: 1. Obtain current frontier set from FrontierGenerationModule. 2. If frontier set empty: - Return invalid SelectedTarget; mission control may consider exploration finished. 3. For each frontier cell f: a. Compute travel-time component: - Use PathPlanner heuristic cost or full path cost with Path Optimality Criterion. b. Estimate "information gain" term: - Count UNKNOWN cells within k-step Manhattan neighborhood (k configurable, e.g., 2) of frontier f that are not blocked by PRESENT walls. c. Combine: heuristic(f) = travel_time_weight * travel_time(f) - info_gain_weight * expected_new_cells(f) travel_time_weight and info_gain_weight configurable. d. Estimate explore time requirement: - required_time_ms(f) ≈ (path_length_in_cells(f) + exploration_margin_cells) * time_per_cell_ms - Additional margin accounts for turns and sensor stops. e. Discard candidate if required_time_ms(f) > remaining_exploration_time_ms. 4. Among remaining candidates, select frontier with minimal heuristic(f). Break ties deterministically (e.g., by lexicographic (row, col)). 5. Return SelectedTarget with chosen frontier and associated metrics.
Component.Timing: - Target selection may be called when: * No active exploration path. * Current frontier target reached or becomes invalid. - Complexity: O(F) over frontier cells, acceptable for 32-cell maze.
Component.Invariants: - Only frontier cells that meet remaining-time constraint are considered. - Heuristic evaluation uses same traversal cost model as defined for path optimality (consistency with planner).
Component.Failures: - No candidate meets time constraint: * Handling: SelectedTarget marked invalid; MissionControlStateMachine may conclude exploration based on time limit or map completeness.

Component.Name: PathPlanner
Component.Purpose: Implements cell-based path planning on the 4x8 grid map using a graph search algorithm (e.g., Dijkstra or A*). Nodes are FREE cells; edges link 4-neighbor FREE cells not separated by PRESENT walls. Provides planning for exploration frontiers, return-to-start, and second run; supports optimal path cost definition and replanning upon map updates; signals planning failures when no path exists.

Signal.Name: MapReadRequests
Signal.Description: Synchronous requests to query cell and wall classification via: - get_cell_state(row,col) - get_wall_state_between(cell_a, cell_b) - enumeration of neighbor cells (up,down,left,right).

Signal.Name: PathResult
Signal.Description: Struct { bool success; vector<Cell> cells; float total_cost; }: - success=false indicates planning failure. - cells contains ordered cell coordinates from start to target/goal cell.

Signal.Name: PlanToFrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Inputs: current_cell, target_frontier_cell, traversal_cost_profile (exploration). Output: PathResult.

Signal.Name: PlanToStartRequest
Signal.Description: Inputs: current_cell, start_cell. Output: PathResult.

Signal.Name: PlanToGoalRegionRequest
Signal.Description: Inputs: start_cell, goal_region (set of up to four cells). Output: PathResult corresponding to best goal cell.

Signal.Name: GoalCostQueries
Signal.Description: Provides path cost evaluation from start_cell to each candidate 2x2 FREE region's cells to determine minimum cost region and cell. Uses same path optimality criterion.

Signal.Name: CurrentPathForExecution
Signal.Description: After computing a path, PathPlanner (or MissionControl) passes path to MotionControlModule. The path is a contiguous list of FREE cells; no step crosses PRESENT walls or enters BLOCKED cells.

Component.Execution: - Graph Construction: * Implicit graph, not stored explicitly: for any cell c, its neighbors are cells with |dr|+|dc|=1 within bounds, provided: - neighbor cell classified FREE - wall state between c and neighbor is not PRESENT. - Cost Model: * Each edge represents moving from one FREE cell to orthogonally adjacent FREE cell. * Base cost = straight_travel_cost (configurable, e.g., 1.0). * Additional turn penalty when heading between consecutive steps differs: - For search not maintaining heading, approximate number_of_turns along path; alternatively, include heading dimension in state. * total_path_cost = Σ(edge_costs). * For second run, configure cost parameters to approximate physical time: cost_per_cell proportional to nominal traversal time, turn penalty adjusted for deceleration/acceleration overhead. - Algorithm: * Use Dijkstra or A* search: - Node: cell (row,col), optionally with heading. - Priority queue ordered by (g_cost + h_cost). - Heuristic h for A*: e.g., Manhattan distance * straight_travel_cost. * For PlanToGoalRegionRequest: - Introduce virtual goal node connecting to each FREE goal cell with zero-cost edge; compute min cost; then choose corresponding physical goal cell. - Replanning: * When notified from GridMapModule or MissionControlStateMachine that map update invalidates current path (FREE->BLOCKED or ABSENT->PRESENT on path edges): - Re-run planning from robot current cell to original target/goal. - If failure, signal planning failure to MissionControlStateMachine. - Planning Failure: * If open set becomes empty without reaching any target goal cell, mark success=false and signal PlanningFailure.
Component.Timing: - Grid size 4x8, maximum 32 nodes, edges ≤ 2*32. Dijkstra/A* completes within a single control-loop iteration on typical embedded MCU. - For second run, algorithm can be run once before starting fast execution.
Component.Invariants: - No path returned includes cells classified BLOCKED or edges across PRESENT walls at planning time. - Path costs always non-negative; algorithm ensures computed path has minimal total_traversal_cost among feasible paths under current cost parameters.
Component.Failures: - No path exists: * Handling: PathResult.success=false, PlanningFailure event/flag set. Consumers (MissionControl, ReturnToStartService, SecondRunPlanningService) react appropriately. - Inconsistent map (e.g., start or goal not FREE): * Handling: treat as planning failure; may require higher-level error handling.

Component.Name: ReturnToStartService
Component.Purpose: Provides return-to-start behavior. On receiving EXPLORATION_COMPLETED or EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event, computes and executes a path from the robot's current cell to the designated start cell using the PathPlanner and MotionControlModule. Monitors execution and requests replanning if path invalidated. Signals failures to MissionControlStateMachine.

Signal.Name: ReturnPathStatus
Signal.Description: Enum {IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESS, FAILURE}. SUCCESS when robot reaches start cell. FAILURE when planner cannot find path or repeated replanning fails. MissionControlStateMachine uses to manage RETURN_TO_START and trigger SECOND_RUN or mission termination.

Signal.Name: ReturnToStartTrigger
Signal.Description: Includes: - cause: {EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT} - current_cell from pose estimation. Called when MissionControlStateMachine enters RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: ReturnPath
Signal.Description: Planned path from current cell to start cell (see PathPlanner.PathResult). Contains success flag and path.

Signal.Name: PlanToStartRequest
Signal.Description: Inputs: current_cell, start_cell taken from GridMapModule. Execution same as normal PathPlanner request.

Signal.Name: ReturnMotionCommand
Signal.Description: Motion execution request for the return path; mode=RETURN, path=ReturnPath.cells.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Indicates if robot has reached start cell or if execution aborted/requires replanning.

Signal.Name: EXPLORATION_COMPLETED_or_TIMEOUT
Signal.Description: Event consumed indirectly via MissionControl; cause of invoking ReturnToStartService.

Component.Execution: - On ReturnToStartTrigger: 1. Query current_cell from PoseEstimationModule and start_cell from GridMapModule. 2. Request PlanToStartRequest from PathPlanner. 3. If planning successful: - Send ReturnMotionCommand to MotionControlModule. - Set internal state to IN_PROGRESS. 4. If planning failure: - Set ReturnPathStatus=FAILURE. Inform MissionControlStateMachine. - While IN_PROGRESS: - Monitor PathExecutionStatus each control loop: * If reached_target and target==start_cell: - Set ReturnPathStatus=SUCCESS. * If aborted due to updated map or emergency: - Request re-plan from current_cell to start_cell. - If repeated planning failures or repeated aborts beyond threshold: - Set ReturnPathStatus=FAILURE.
Component.Timing: - ReturnToStartService's planning and monitoring are called each control-loop via MissionControlStateMachine orchestration. - Actual motion durations depend on path length and motion primitives; no additional periodic tasks beyond control-loop updates.
Component.Invariants: - During RETURN_TO_START, robot never commanded into BLOCKED cells or across PRESENT walls. - Service uses same traversal cost model as primary planner, but priority is guaranteed reachability, not speed.
Component.Failures: - No path to start: * Handling: immediate FAILURE, MissionControlStateMachine transitions to COMPLETED. - Persistent aborts (e.g., repeated emergency stops) preventing progress: * Handling: after configurable retry limit, return FAILURE.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--RUN--1.1.1 LL--RUN--1.1.2 LL--MAP--1.3.4 LL--PLAN--1.1.1 LL--EVENT--1.1.1 LL--INTERF--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2

Component.Name: SecondRunPlanningService
Component.Purpose: Manages second-run fastest-path behavior: triggered when robot is back at start and exploration is complete. Computes candidate goal regions, selects optimal one, derives fastest path from start to selected region using tuned traversal cost model, commands MotionControlModule to execute path at higher speeds while respecting safety constraints, and produces success/failure status.

Signal.Name: SecondRunStatus
Signal.Description: Enum {SUCCESS, FAILURE} plus reason fields. SUCCESS when robot reaches any FREE cell in goal region without collision; FAILURE otherwise. Latched by MissionControlStateMachine in COMPLETED.

Signal.Name: SecondRunStartTrigger
Signal.Description: Indication when MissionControlStateMachine transitions to SECOND_RUN state. Preconditions: robot at start cell; exploration declared complete. Includes start_cell from GridMapModule and exploration_complete_flag.

Signal.Name: MapSnapshotForGoals
Signal.Description: Provides FREE/BLOCKED classification of all cells for goal-region identification, plus start_cell coordinate.

Signal.Name: SelectedGoalRegion
Signal.Description: A 2x2 contiguous block of FREE cells and chosen goal cell (for path end) that minimizes path cost from start, using PathPlanner traversal cost metric. Provided to SecondRunPlanningService.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionSelectionRequest
Signal.Description: Inputs: start_cell, map snapshot. Returns SelectedGoalRegion.

Signal.Name: SecondRunPath
Signal.Description: Optimal path from start_cell to chosen goal cell using traversal cost parameters tuned for time minimization (e.g., lower straight cost, tuned turn penalty).

Signal.Name: PlanToGoalRegionRequest
Signal.Description: Called after goal region selection, specifying start and target cells and traversal cost profile specifically for second run (fastest-path profile).

Signal.Name: SecondRunMotionCommand
Signal.Description: Execution command for fastest path: path cells, mode=SECOND_RUN, and reference speed profile (higher speeds within safety).

Signal.Name: EmergencyStopEvent
Signal.Description: Event from MotionControlModule indicating emergency stop triggered due to imminent collision during second run. Causes immediate run failure.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Contains current cell, reached_target, and collision flags. Used to determine success or failure.

Component.Execution: - On SecondRunStartTrigger: 1. Confirm robot pose at start_cell via PoseEstimationModule. 2. Request GoalRegionSelectionRequest from GoalRegionSelector. 3. If no candidate goal region: - Mark SecondRunStatus=FAILURE (no valid goal region); return. 4. Request PlanToGoalRegionRequest from PathPlanner with fastest-path traversal cost profile. 5. If planning fails: - Mark SecondRunStatus=FAILURE; return. 6. Send SecondRunMotionCommand to MotionControlModule with: - path - target speed profile derived from SpeedLimitationForSafety and Fast Path Motion Profile (higher than exploration). - While motion executing: - Monitor PathExecutionStatus each control loop: * If reached_target for any FREE cell in selected 2x2 region and no collision flag: - Mark SecondRunStatus=SUCCESS. * If collision flag set or EmergencyStopEvent occurs that cannot be safely resolved (e.g., repeated stops): - Mark SecondRunStatus=FAILURE. - Instruct MotionControlModule to stop.
Component.Timing: - Planning overhead minimal relative to run time. Single path-planning operation before movement. - Execution speeds higher than exploration but bounded by safety computation (SpeedLimitationForSafety).
Component.Invariants: - Second run only initiated after ReturnToStartService status SUCCESS and map completeness declared. - Fastest-path cost profile always consistent with SpeedLimitationForSafety and EmergencyStop constraints.
Component.Failures: - No reachable 2x2 FREE goal region: * Handling: immediate FAILURE result. - Planner cannot find path to selected goal: * Handling: FAILURE; mission ends. - Any detected collision or unrecoverable emergency stop during path: * Handling: FAILURE; MotionControlModule commanded to stop.

Component.Name: ExplorationStatusModule
Component.Purpose: Tracks exploration progress and time constraints. Uses map completeness flag from GridMapModule and elapsed time since exploration start to publish EXPLORATION_COMPLETED and EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT events. Integrated with MissionControlStateMachine and ReturnToStartService to gate mission transitions.

Signal.Name: MapCompleteFlag
Signal.Description: Boolean from GridMapModule used to determine EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event posting.

Signal.Name: CurrentMissionTime
Signal.Description: Time since exploration start in ms (or global time from boot), used to detect when 5-minute exploration limit is reached.

Signal.Name: EXPLORATION_COMPLETED
Signal.Description: Event enqueued once when map_complete becomes true during EXPLORE. Used by MissionControlStateMachine to transition to RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT
Signal.Description: Event enqueued once when elapsed exploration time >= 5 minutes (300000 ms) while still in EXPLORE. Used to force RETURN_TO_START regardless of completeness.

Signal.Name: ExplorationStartNotification
Signal.Description: Notification when MissionControlStateMachine enters EXPLORE; contains start_time_ms, used as reference for timeout calculation.

Signal.Name: ExplorationEventRecord
Signal.Description: Struct { type, timestamp_ms }. Type ∈ {EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT}. Pushed to single-producer event queue accessible to MissionControl and ReturnToStartService.

Component.Execution: - On ExplorationStartNotification: * Record exploration_start_time_ms. * Clear any previous exploration events. - Each control-loop cycle while in EXPLORE: 1. Compute elapsed_time_ms = now_ms - exploration_start_time_ms. 2. If map_complete==true and EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event not yet issued: - Enqueue EXPLORATION_COMPLETED with timestamp. 3. If elapsed_time_ms >= 300000 ms and EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event not yet issued: - Enqueue EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT with timestamp. 4. Once either event emitted, no further exploration events are generated for current mission.
Component.Timing: - Resolution of timeout detection is one control-loop period (T). - Exploration events delivered upstream within same or next cycle via event queue polling.
Component.Invariants: - At most one EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event per mission. - At most one EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event per mission. - Both events cannot be issued in the same cycle; whichever condition is detected first is considered primary.
Component.Failures: - If both time limit and completeness become true between cycles: * Handling: precedence rule configurable; default: completeness checked before timeout, so EXPLORATION_COMPLETED emitted if both conditions true in same cycle. This implements "whichever is signaled first by corresponding modules".

Component.Name: SensorAbstractionLayer
Component.Purpose: Wraps low-level ultrasonic and infrared hardware interface functions and provides normalized, validated, and filtered sensor data to higher-level modules each control-loop cycle. Handles invalid readings, temporal filtering, and timestamping.

Signal.Name: RawUltrasonicDistance
Signal.Description: float returned by ultrasonic_get_distance(); interpreted as centimeters to nearest front obstacle along beam. May include special invalid values.

Signal.Name: RawIRStatusLeft
Signal.Description: uint8 returned by infrared_get_dsl_status(), interpreted as binary obstacle flag (0=no obstacle, nonzero=obstacle).

Signal.Name: RawIRStatusRight
Signal.Description: uint8 returned by infrared_get_dsr_status().

Signal.Name: SensorFrame
FilteredUltrasonic
Signal.Description: Filtered and validated ultrasonic measurement for use in front wall detection, wall proximity thresholding, and collision risk evaluation. Contains distance_cm and variance estimate.

Signal.Name: FilteredIRFlags
Signal.Description: Debounced left/right IR flags aggregated over small window to suppress single-sample noise.

Signal.Name: SamplingTick
Signal.Description: Periodic callback each control-loop cycle. SensorAbstractionLayer uses it to trigger one sampling of all sensors at configured sampling rate in [10,50] Hz (default 20 Hz).

Component.Execution: - Configuration: * sampling_rate_hz in [10, 50] (default 20). * filter_window_size for moving average or median (e.g., 3-5 samples). * invalid_value_thresholds: negative distances, saturations, etc. - On each SamplingTick: 1. Call ultrasonic_get_distance(); call infrared_get_dsl_status(); infrared_get_dsr_status(). 2. Validate readings: - If ultrasonic distance <= 0 or beyond hardware max or equals known error codes: * Mark reading invalid; optionally substitute with safe_default_distance (e.g., max_range_cm). - For IR: normalize to bool (nonzero means true). 3. Apply temporal filtering: - Maintain circular buffer of last N valid ultrasonic distances; compute filtered value via moving average or median before output. - IR flags may be debounced over small number of samples or majority vote if quickly changing. 4. Construct SensorFrame; set valid flags where necessary (invalid ultrasonic or IR values may be flagged invalid but still provided). 5. Publish SensorFrame to subscribers (MissionControl, WallDetection).
Component.Timing: - Sampling and basic filtering executed once per control loop, ensuring sample rate == control loop frequency (LL--RELIAB--1.2.1). - Timing jitter less than configured tolerance must be ensured by Scheduler.
Component.Invariants: - No downstream mapping or control logic receives raw invalid sensor readings; they are either flagged invalid or replaced with safe defaults. - SensorFrame timestamps monotonically non-decreasing.
Component.Failures: - Persistent invalid readings: * Handling: mark ultrasonic/IR as invalid; can lead to degraded mapping; upper layers may fall back to conservative behavior (e.g., slower speeds, no exploration start).
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--1.1.1 LL--INTERF--1.1.2 LL--INTERF--2.1.1 LL--INTERF--3.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2

Component.Name: WallDetectionModule
Component.Purpose: Interprets filtered ultrasonic and infrared sensor data to detect front and side walls relative to current cell and robot pose. Applies uncertainty, angular alignment checks, hysteresis, and consistency checks before committing wall presence/absence updates to GridMapModule. Triggers wall avoidance if current trajectory intersects detected walls within one cell length.

Signal.Name: FilteredUltrasonic
Signal.Description: Filtered ultrasonic distance measurement (distance_cm ± uncertainty).

Signal.Name: FilteredIRFlags
Signal.Description: Left/right binary obstacle flags.

Signal.Name: CurrentPose
Signal.Description: Discrete cell (row,col), approximate continuous pose (x,y,θ), and heading alignment flag (aligned_with_maze_axes) with tolerance <=10 degrees.

Signal.Name: WallObservation
Signal.Description: Observations for front and side faces: - front: derived from ultrasonic intersection interval. - sides: derived from IR obstacle indication and orientation compensation.

Signal.Name: ExistingMapClassification
Signal.Description: Query existing wall and cell classifications for consistency checks.

Signal.Name: AvoidanceRequest
Signal.Description: Request to perform avoidance maneuver when a detected front/side wall lies within one cell length along current trajectory; includes recommended maneuver type.

Signal.Name: HysteresisAndConsistencyConfig
Signal.Description: Configurable thresholds for distance hysteresis and consistency checks (e.g., N consecutive readings required).

Component.Execution: - Front Wall Detection From Ultrasonic: 1. For each FilteredUltrasonic distance d: - Construct interval I_meas = [d - 12 cm, d + 12 cm] (per ultrasonic uncertainty model). - Compute expected robot-center-to-wall position along front direction for current cell using: expected_distance = (cell_size_cm / 2) - robot_radius_cm - 0.5 * wall_thickness_cm, adjusted by pose offset. - Define expected interval I_cell = [expected_distance - tolerance, expected_distance + tolerance]. - If I_meas intersects I_cell and passes proximity threshold/hysteresis, interpret as front wall PRESENT on front boundary of current cell. - Side Wall Detection From IR: 1. When left_flag or right_flag active and pose aligned_with_maze_axes within <=10 degrees: - Use IR effective range (approx. 4 cm) and 25-degree mounting angle to project obstacle point to lateral wall position in maze coordinates. - If projection consistent with side wall of current cell within clearance margins, mark corresponding side wall PRESENT. - Wall Proximity Thresholding: 1. Apply configurable thresholds (distance_to_wall < threshold_cm) and hysteresis windows to avoid toggling between wall and free states. 2. Use filtered ultrasonic to detect free space beyond threshold to classify ABSENT if consistent. - Consistency and Map Update: 1. For each tentative wall PRESENT/ABSENT detection: - Compare with existing map classification via GridMapModule. - If conflicting with established FREE/ABSENT or PRESENT: * Require N consecutive consistent readings (N>=2, default 3) before overwriting; however, static map assumption disallows actual state change, so in practice classification remains as first observation, and later conflicting readings may be ignored or flagged as sensor inconsistency. 2. Generate WallObservation messages for GridMapModule when confidence threshold reached and allowed by immutability rules. - Wall Avoidance: 1. Predict current robot trajectory for one cell length using current velocity and pose. 2. If front/side detected wall lies within this trajectory envelope without minimum clearance margin M: - Issue AvoidanceRequest to MotionControlModule with chosen maneuver type: * STOP_AND_TURN (stop, rotate away from wall, continue). * ARC_TURN (reduce inner wheel speed to curve around). - Maneuver selection respects robot footprint and clearance M>0 so that executed path respects clearance constraints.
Component.Timing: - Wall detection invoked every control-loop cycle. - Avoidance decisions made within a single cycle after detection to ensure enough braking distance, consistent with EmergencyStop thresholds.
Component.Invariants: - Wall detections are only trusted when sensor pose alignment conditions are met (for side walls). - Once wall PRESENT/ABSENT classification established in map, subsequent conflicting evidence is treated as sensor noise and does not revert classification.
Component.Failures: - Incorrect detection due to noise: * Mitigated via filtering, hysteresis, and consistency threshold N. - Failure to detect immediate wall leads to EmergencyStop logic as final safety net.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--MAP--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.2.2 LL--MAP--1.2.3 LL--MAP--1.2.4 LL--MAP--1.3.2 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2 LL--INTERF--2.1.1 LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.1.1 LL--INTERF--3.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.4.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--ENV--1.3.1

Component.Name: MotionControlModule
Component.Purpose: Translates high-level paths and avoidance/emergency-stop commands into sequences of motor_set(left_speed, right_speed) calls. Implements differential-drive kinematics-based cell-center motion primitives, speed limitation for safety, speed command rate limiting, emergency stop handling, and wall clearance enforcement in execution.

Signal.Name: motor_set
Signal.Description: Function motor_set(uint8 left_speed, uint8 right_speed) where speed∈[0,100]. Called at most once per control-loop iteration. Commands normalized speed for each wheel.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Execution feedback: { current_cell, segment_index, reached_target, aborted }.

Signal.Name: SecondRunStatusInput
Signal.Description: For second run, MotionControlModule reports any collision or emergency-stop that prevented successful completion.

Signal.Name: MotionCommand
Signal.Description: Contains: path (cell list), mode {EXPLORE, RETURN, SECOND_RUN}, and optional desired speed scaling.

Signal.Name: StopCommand
Signal.Description: Instructs immediate stop of planned motion, decelerating to 0 using allowed rate limit or immediate if emergency.

Signal.Name: EmergencyStopEvent
Signal.Description: Generated by MotionControlModule when EmergencyStop threshold triggered; mirrored to other modules that care about emergency conditions.

Signal.Name: AvoidanceRequest
Signal.Description: Contains avoidance maneuver type and parameters. MotionControlModule converts this into actual speed sequences.

Signal.Name: FilteredUltrasonicForEmergency
Signal.Description: Used to compute distance to obstacle and decide EmergencyStop based on current speed and emergency distance threshold.

Signal.Name: ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Periodic callback used to execute one step of active motion primitive and to optionally call motor_set() once.

Signal.Name: CommandedSpeeds
Signal.Description: Provided to PoseEstimationModule to integrate differential-drive kinematics and update pose estimation.

Component.Execution: - Configuration: * max_safe_speed_cm_s (computed from SpeedLimitationForSafety constraints). * exploration_speed_scale <= max_safe_speed_cm_s. * second_run_speed_scale (higher but <= max_safe_speed_cm_s). * speed_increment_limit_per_cycle for each motor (for rate limiting). * emergency_distance_threshold_cm. - Motion Primitive Model: * Forward one-cell primitive: - Compute required time to traverse cell_size_cm at selected translational speed. - Convert translational speed to motor_set speeds based on robot geometry and baseline calibration. * Turn-in-place primitive: - Set left and right speeds to equal and opposite values to achieve 90-degree or 180-degree rotations. - Control Loop Step: 1. If StopCommand received: - Gradually or immediately (if flagged as emergency) reduce left/right speeds to 0 respecting rate limiting. 2. If new MotionCommand received: - Construct sequence of primitives based on path (cell transitions plus necessary turns). - Set current primitive index to 0; set mode accordingly (EXPLORE/RETURN/SECOND_RUN). 3. If active primitive: - Compute target motor speeds for this cycle. - Apply speed limitation: * Clip speeds such that resulting translational speed ≤ max_safe_speed_cm_s. - Apply rate limiting: * Ensure change from last commanded speed does not exceed per-cycle increment. - Check EmergencyStop: * Using FilteredUltrasonicForEmergency and current speed, compute nominal stopping distance. * If obstacle detected closer than emergency_distance_threshold_cm < stopping distance: - Immediately command motor_set(0,0). - Raise EmergencyStopEvent; mark aborted=true. - Otherwise call motor_set(left_speed, right_speed). 4. Update PathExecutionStatus: - Using PoseEstimationModule's current cell: * If equals last path cell: reached_target=true. * If aborted or EmergencyStopEvent: aborted=true.
Component.Timing: - motor_set called at most once per control-loop iteration (LL--INTERF--1.2.1). - EmergencyStop checks run each cycle with full updated sensor data at 10-50 Hz.
Component.Invariants: - Commanded speeds always within [0,100]. - Rate of change of speeds per cycle never exceeds configured increment (except when emergency immediate zeroing is allowed and documented as exception). - Planned trajectories always respect minimum clearance M when following ideal commands.
Component.Failures: - EmergencyStop triggered: * Handling: motors commanded to 0 immediately; aborted flag; upstream modules replan or abort run. - If repeated emergency stops on same segment: * Handling: MotionControlModule may escalate to permanent aborted status for current MotionCommand; mission-level logic decides next steps.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--1.2.1 LL--INTERF--1.2.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.3.1 LL--RUN--1.1.2 LL--RUN--1.2.3 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--MAP--1.2.4

Component.Name: PoseEstimationModule
Component.Purpose: Maintains the robot's estimated discrete cell index and heading aligned with maze axes, integrating differential-drive kinematics and continuous pose over control-loop periods. Emits cell-center entry notifications and performs corrections based on wall detections and map features.

Signal.Name: CommandedSpeeds
Signal.Description: Left and right wheel speeds commanded in last motor_set call, plus control-loop period T or measured cycle time. Used for dead-reckoning integration.

Signal.Name: CycleDuration
Signal.Description: Measured cycle duration Δt between control-loop ticks; if unavailable, configured period T is used.

Signal.Name: CellCenterTransitionEvent
Signal.Description: Emitted when predicted pose crosses from one cell center to adjacent cell center; includes from_cell, to_cell, and transition direction.

Signal.Name: PoseForWallDetection
Signal.Description: Provides current (row,col,heading,continuous pose) and alignment flags to be used in wall detection.

Signal.Name: WallAlignmentCorrectionRequest
Signal.Description: Encapsulates wall-based pose correction suggestions; PoseEstimationModule uses to snap pose to nearest axis and cell center consistent with walls.

Component.Execution: - Continuous Pose Update: 1. For each control-loop cycle: - Use differential drive kinematic equations: v_left, v_right from commanded speeds, wheel radius r, and baseline B. v = (v_right + v_left)/2, ω = (v_right - v_left)/B. x_new = x_old + v * cos(θ) * Δt. y_new = y_old + v * sin(θ) * Δt. θ_new = θ_old + ω * Δt. 2. Map continuous (x,y) to grid cell indices using grid coordinate system: - row = floor(y / cell_size_cm). - col = floor(x / cell_size_cm). 3. Maintain discrete heading as nearest axis-aligned direction (N,E,S,W) from θ, used in mapping and planning. - Cell-Center Entry Detection: 1. Define nominal center positions for each cell. 2. When continuous position crosses midpoint between cells along cardinal direction and enters capture radius of new cell center: - Emit CellCenterTransitionEvent and update current cell indices. - Pose Correction Using Map Features: 1. When robot is near cell center and WallDetectionModule indicates proximity to known walls aligned with maze structure: - Snap θ to nearest multiple of 90 degrees. - Adjust (x,y) to nearest cell center consistent with wall distances (front/side). 2. This correction resets integration drift to maintain alignment with discrete map.
Component.Timing: - Executed each control-loop cycle with same period as MotionControlModule. - Pose corrections applied opportunistically during axis alignment conditions.
Component.Invariants: - Discrete (row,col) always within [0..3]x[0..7] while robot remains in maze bounds. - Discrete heading always one of {N,E,S,W}.
Component.Failures: - Pose divergence due to wheel slip or incorrect kinematics: * Mitigation: frequent correction at cell centers using observed walls.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--POSE--1.1.1 LL--POSE--1.1.2 LL--POSE--1.1.3 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.1 LL--ENV--1.1.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.3.2

Component.Name: InitializationModule
Component.Purpose: Performs basic hardware and software initialization: sets up sensor and motor interfaces, initializes GridMapModule (unknown map, start cell FREE), PoseEstimationModule, MotionControlModule, Scheduler parameters, and mission-level structures. Signals INIT_COMPLETED to allow MissionControlStateMachine to exit IDLE and start exploration.

Signal.Name: INIT_COMPLETED
Signal.Description: Flag or event set once when initialization finished. MissionControlStateMachine polls or subscribes to this to transition IDLE->EXPLORE when also first valid SensorFrame present.

Signal.Name: MapInitializationCall
Signal.Description: Call to reset map, set all cells and walls to UNKNOWN, then set start cell FREE and store its coordinate.

Signal.Name: SchedulerSetup
Signal.Description: Configuration of control-loop period T and sensor sampling rate equal to T.

Signal.Name: SensorInit
Signal.Description: Configures sampling frequency, filtering parameters, and resets internal buffers.

Signal.Name: MotionInit
Signal.Description: Configures speed limits, rate limiting, and emergency thresholds.

Component.Execution: - On system boot: 1. Initialize hardware drivers for ultrasonic, IR, motors, and timers. 2. Call MapInitializationCall on GridMapModule: - Set cell/wall states to UNKNOWN. - Set start cell coordinate and mark as FREE. 3. Initialize PoseEstimationModule with start pose at start cell center and heading aligned with chosen axis. 4. Configure Scheduler period T and start periodic control loop. 5. Configure SensorAbstractionLayer sampling and filtering. 6. Configure MotionControlModule parameters. 7. After all steps succeed and first valid sensor readings can be obtained, set INIT_COMPLETED flag/event in main context.
Component.Timing: - One-time execution at startup before entering control-loop-driven mission operations.
Component.Invariants: - INIT_COMPLETED set exactly once per boot. - No mission states other than IDLE executed prior to INIT_COMPLETED.
Component.Failures: - If any subcomponent initialization fails: * Handling: do not set INIT_COMPLETED; system remains in safe state; possible error reporting outside this design scope.

Component.Name: ReliabilityModule
Component.Purpose: Encapsulates sensor noise handling, data consistency checks, minimum clearance management, and emergency-stop policy tuning. Supplies parameters and logic used by SensorAbstractionLayer, WallDetectionModule, MotionControlModule, and GridMapModule to ensure reliable operation under noisy measurements and uncertain environment.

Signal.Name: SamplingAndFilteringConfig
Signal.Description: Configurable sampling rate, filter window sizes, and allowable timing jitter tolerance used by sensor abstraction.

Signal.Name: ConsistencyThresholdConfig
Signal.Description: N (>=2, default 3, suggested 2≤N≤10) consecutive readings required before overriding existing map classification for contradictory wall evidence.

Signal.Name: HysteresisConfig
Signal.Description: Thresholds for distinguishing wall vs free based on ultrasonic distance, including hysteresis band to avoid flip-flop.

Signal.Name: ClearanceAndSpeedConstraints
Signal.Description: Minimum clearance M>0 cm and derived max_safe_speed_cm_s based on ultrasonic uncertainty and emergency stop policy. Provided to MotionControl for enforcement in planning and execution.

Signal.Name: EmergencyStopThreshold
Signal.Description: Distance threshold for EmergencyStop: must be less than nominal stopping distance given current speed and sampling rate so robot can stop before wall.

Signal.Name: ConsistencyPolicy
Signal.Description: Rules for when conflicting sensor readings can cause map changes; primarily ensures static map assumption, thus no override after initial classification.

Component.Execution: - At startup: 1. Configure sampling rate, default N, hysteresis thresholds, minimum clearance M, and default emergency stop distance based on ultrasonic uncertainty (±12 cm) and hardware limits. 2. Compute max_safe_speed_cm_s to satisfy: - At current sampling rate and worst-case uncertainty, robot can stop before reaching detected wall if EmergencyStop triggered at threshold distance. - At runtime: - Provide these parameters via getters or direct configuration structures to relevant modules. - Perform optional runtime monitoring (e.g., verifying jitter constraints).
Component.Invariants: - chosen N always >=2. - chosen M>0 and consistent with ultrasonic uncertainty model.
Component.Failures: - Misconfiguration leading to impossible constraints: * Handling: clamp parameters to safe ranges; report configuration error.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2 LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.3.1

State.Name: IDLE
State.StateAction: - Wait for INIT_COMPLETED flag/event from InitializationModule. - Monitor SensorFrame validity; ensure at least one valid cycle of ultrasonic and IR measurements observed. - Do not issue any MotionCommand; ensure motors remain stopped (StopCommand if necessary).

State.Name: EXPLORE
State.StateAction: - If no active exploration target: 1. Request frontier set from FrontierGenerationModule. 2. Request target selection from ExplorationTargetSelector, providing remaining exploration time. 3. If valid target: - Request path to target from PathPlanner (exploration cost profile). - If planning successful: send MotionCommand(mode=EXPLORE, path) to MotionControlModule. - If planning fails: treat as lack of reachable frontiers; may cause EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event. - While path executing: 1. Monitor PathExecutionStatus to detect target reached or aborted. 2. If target reached: invalidate active target; loop to select next target. 3. If aborted due to emergency or invalid path: request replanning to target or select new target if frontier changed. - Continuously check for EXPLORATION_COMPLETED or EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT events from ExplorationStatusModule; do not start new targets after such events.

State.Name: RETURN_TO_START
State.StateAction: - On entry, invoke ReturnToStartService with cause (COMPLETED or TIMEOUT). - Monitor ReturnPathStatus: * IN_PROGRESS: no action; allow MotionControlModule to execute. * SUCCESS: trigger SECOND_RUN state if configured, else COMPLETED. * FAILURE: transition directly to COMPLETED with failure flag.

State.Name: SECOND_RUN
State.StateAction: - On entry: 1. Request SecondRunPlanningService to compute fastest path from start to selected goal region. 2. If planning fails: set second_run_status=FAILURE and transition to COMPLETED. - While executing path: 1. Monitor SecondRunPlanningService and MotionControlModule status for completion, emergency stops, collisions. 2. When ANY goal cell in 2x2 region reached without collisions: set second_run_status=SUCCESS and transition to COMPLETED. 3. If any collision or unrecoverable abort: set second_run_status=FAILURE and transition to COMPLETED.

State.Name: COMPLETED
State.StateAction: - Immediately issue StopCommand to MotionControlModule to ensure no new motion. - Latch final mission outcome: * exploration_cause ∈ {MAP_COMPLETE, TIMEOUT, NO_FRONTIERS}. * return_to_start_status ∈ {SUCCESS, FAILURE, NOT_EXECUTED}. * second_run_status ∈ {SUCCESS, FAILURE, NOT_EXECUTED}. - Provide status to external reporting interface; remain in this state indefinitely.

Transition.Trigger: - INIT_COMPLETED flag set AND at least one valid SensorFrame with ultrasonic and IR data has been received in current or prior control-loop iteration.
Transition.Guard: - No other fatal initialization error recorded.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Notify ExplorationStatusModule of exploration start, recording current time as exploration_start_time_ms.

Transition.Trigger: - Receipt of EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event from ExplorationStatusModule OR - Receipt of EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event from ExplorationStatusModule.
Transition.Guard: - None; first event received triggers transition.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Invalidate any active exploration target. - Issue StopCommand to MotionControlModule if a path is actively executing. - Invoke ReturnToStartService with parameter cause = COMPLETED if EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, or TIMEOUT if EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT.

Transition.Trigger: - ReturnPathStatus == SUCCESS AND exploration previously declared complete (EXPLORATION_COMPLETED) AND second-run functionality enabled.
Transition.Guard: - PoseEstimationModule confirms current cell == start_cell coordinate.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Notify SecondRunPlanningService of SECOND_RUN start; pass start_cell and goal region information.

Transition.Trigger: - ReturnPathStatus == FAILURE OR second-run functionality disabled.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Record return_to_start_status = FAILURE or NOT_EXECUTED. - Issue StopCommand to MotionControlModule.

Transition.Trigger: - SecondRunStatus == SUCCESS OR SecondRunStatus == FAILURE, as reported by SecondRunPlanningService based on MotionControlModule feedback.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Issue StopCommand to MotionControlModule. - Latch second_run_status and expose status externally.

Data.Content:
Grid coordinate system and geometry:
- Maze size: * rows = 4, columns = 8.
- Coordinate convention: * row index increases from bottom to top or as defined; adopt a consistent convention: row 0 at one fixed maze edge (e.g., "south"), row 3 at opposite edge ("north"); column 0 at "west" side, column 7 at "east" side. * Start cell is at (row=0, col=0).
- Cell geometry: * cell_size_cm ≈ 17.2 cm. * cell center coordinates in maze frame: x_center = (col + 0.5) * cell_size_cm; y_center = (row + 0.5) * cell_size_cm.
- Wall geometry: * wall boundaries along cell edges, indexed as: - vertical walls: between (row, col) and (row, col+1); - horizontal walls: between (row, col) and (row+1, col). * wall_thickness_cm ≈ 1.6 cm (used for clearance and ultrasonic matching).
- Robot geometry: * Robot modeled as circle with radius robot_radius_cm = 5.5 cm. * Minimum clearance margin M (cm) used to inflate walls by M around robot footprint for planning and collision checking.

Time management parameters:
- control_loop_period_T_ms: * Configurable; must satisfy sensor sampling requirement in [10,50] Hz: T_ms ∈ [20, 100] with default 50 ms (20 Hz).
- global_exploration_time_limit_ms: * 300000 ms (5 minutes) from exploration_start_time.
- nominal_cell_traversal_duration_ms: * Configurable parameter representing nominal time to traverse one cell at exploration speed. * Expressed as uint32; default value derived from experiments (e.g., 800 ms). * Accessible by ExplorationTargetSelector and time-aware planning logic.
- remaining_exploration_time_ms: * Computed each cycle during EXPLORE: remaining_exploration_time_ms = max(0, global_exploration_time_limit_ms - elapsed_time_ms). * Passed into target selection to prune targets that can't be visited in time.

Ultrasonic uncertainty and collision safety computation:
- Ultrasonic uncertainty: * Each measurement d is modeled with interval [d - 12 cm, d + 12 cm].
- Emergency stop policy: * emergency_distance_threshold_cm chosen such that: emergency_distance_threshold_cm ≤ stopping_distance_cm - safety_margin_cm. * stopping_distance_cm estimated based on current speed v and maximum deceleration achievable via motor_set commands and friction.
- Maximum safe speed computation: * Given: sampling_rate_hz, ultrasonic_uncertainty_cm = 12, emergency_distance_threshold_cm, required minimum clearance M. * Ensure: max_safe_speed_cm_s * (1/sampling_rate_hz) + braking_distance(max_safe_speed_cm_s) + uncertainty_margin ≤ observed_wall_distance - M. * This constraint is used to derive upper bound max_safe_speed_cm_s configured in MotionControlModule.
- Clearance enforcement: * PathPlanner and MotionControlModule ensure planned robot center trajectory never enters region within (robot_radius_cm + M + 0.5 * wall_thickness_cm) of any PRESENT wall line.

Exploration event queue structure:
- Event record: struct ExplorationEvent { ExplorationEventType type; // COMPLETED or TIMEOUT uint32 timestamp_ms; // time of event emission };
- SystemEventQueue: * Single-producer (ExplorationStatusModule) single-consumer (MissionControlStateMachine and ReturnToStartService via shared accessor). * Capacity at least 2 to store both COMPLETED and TIMEOUT if they occur, though logic intends to emit only one per mission.
- API: * bool enqueue_event(ExplorationEvent e) - called by ExplorationStatusModule. * bool dequeue_event(ExplorationEvent* out) - polled by MissionControlStateMachine each control loop.

Goal region identification and selection algorithm:
- Identification: 1. For every potential 2x2 block with top-left at (row, col) where row∈[0,2], col∈[0,6]: - Check four cells: (row,col), (row,col+1), (row+1,col), (row+1,col+1). - If all four are FREE in GridMapModule and reachable from start_cell according to PathPlanner: * Add to candidate goal regions list.
- Selection: 1. For each candidate region: - Define its set of goal cells G={g1..g4}. - For each gi∈G: * Compute optimal path cost cost(start_cell, gi) using PathPlanner's traversal cost model. - Determine region cost as min over gi of these costs. 2. Select region with minimal region cost. 3. Provide selected region and target goal cell (the cell achieving minimal path cost) to SecondRunPlanningService.
- Integration: * GoalRegionSelector component implements this algorithm using GridMapModule for map state and PathPlanner for reachability and cost queries.
